q.en: In what year was Julius Caesar born?
q.zh: 尤利乌斯·凯撒出生于哪一年？

options.en: 100 BC
options.zh: 公元前100年

options.en: 44 BC
options.zh: 公元前44年

options.en: 509 BC
options.zh: 公元前509年

options.en: A year long before Rome became a republic
options.zh: 罗马成为共和国之前很久的一年

explain.en: Caesar was born on July 12 or 13 in 100 BC.
explain.zh: 凯撒生于公元前100年7月12日或13日。

q.en: In what year did Caesar declare himself dictator of Rome?
q.zh: 凯撒是在哪一年宣布自己为罗马独裁官的？

options.en: In 100 BC, the year he was born
options.zh: 公元前100年，他出生的那年

options.en: In 46 BC
options.zh: 公元前46年

options.en: In 49 BC, the year he crossed the Rubicon
options.zh: 公元前49年，他渡过卢比孔河的那年

options.en: In 509 BC, the year the Roman Republic began
options.zh: 公元前509年，罗马共和国建立的那年

explain.en: After defeating his rivals, Caesar declared himself dictator in 46 BC.
explain.zh: 击败对手后，凯撒于公元前46年宣布自己为独裁官。

q.en: In the book, what is a 'tactician'?
q.zh: 在书中，“谋略家”（tactician）是什么意思？

options.en: A soldier who only follows orders
options.zh: 只服从命令的士兵

options.en: A writer of long speeches who never went into battle himself
options.zh: 一个从未上过战场、只写长篇演讲的人

options.en: Someone good at carefully planning how to reach a goal
options.zh: 善于精心规划如何实现目标的人

options.en: A person who builds roads for the army
options.zh: 为军队修筑道路的人

explain.en: Caesar was a brilliant tactician, skilled at planning how to win battles.
explain.zh: 凯撒是一位杰出的谋略家，善于规划如何赢得战斗。

q.en: In what year did Caesar cross the Rubicon River and start a civil war?
q.zh: 凯撒是在哪一年渡过卢比孔河、发动内战的？

options.en: In 52 BC, the year he defeated Vercingetorix at Alesia
options.zh: 公元前52年，他在阿莱西亚击败维钦托利的那年

options.en: In 46 BC
options.zh: 公元前46年

options.en: In 44 BC
options.zh: 公元前44年

options.en: In 49 BC
options.zh: 公元前49年

explain.en: In 49 BC Caesar crossed the Rubicon with his army, an act of war that began the civil war.
explain.zh: 公元前49年，凯撒带军队渡过卢比孔河，这一宣战之举开启了内战。

q.en: What happened at the Battle of Alesia in 52 BC?
q.zh: 公元前52年的阿莱西亚之战发生了什么？

options.en: He defeated Vercingetorix and united Gaul
options.zh: 他击败了维钦托利，统一了高卢

options.en: He built the first Roman road
options.zh: 他修建了第一条罗马道路

options.en: He invented the calendar
options.zh: 他发明了历法

options.en: He sailed across the ocean to discover a new continent
options.zh: 他漂洋过海发现了一块新大陆

explain.en: At Alesia, Caesar defeated the Gallic leader Vercingetorix and united Gaul under Roman rule.
explain.zh: 在阿莱西亚，凯撒击败高卢领袖维钦托利，使高卢统一于罗马统治之下。

q.en: On what date was Julius Caesar assassinated?
q.zh: 尤利乌斯·凯撒是在哪一天遇刺的？

options.en: July 4
options.zh: 7月4日

options.en: March 15, 44 BC
options.zh: 公元前44年3月15日

options.en: December 25
A day chosen at random by the book's author
options.zh: 本书作者随便选的一天

explain.en: Senators assassinated Caesar on March 15, 44 BC, known as the Ides of March.
explain.zh: 元老院议员于公元前44年3月15日（即“三月之望”）刺杀了凯撒。

q.en: Which three men formed the First Triumvirate?
q.zh: 哪三个人组成了“前三头同盟”？

options.en: Caesar and Hannibal
options.zh: 凯撒和汉尼拔

options.en: Pompey and Cleopatra
options.zh: 庞培和克娄巴特拉

options.en: Caesar, Pompey, and Crassus
options.zh: 凯撒、庞培和克拉苏

options.en: Three kings who ruled Rome before the Republic began
options.zh: 共和国建立之前统治罗马的三位国王

explain.en: Caesar joined with Pompey and Crassus to form the First Triumvirate, a powerful alliance.
explain.zh: 凯撒与庞培、克拉苏联手，组成了强大的“前三头同盟”。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing about Julius Caesar?
q.zh: 作者写尤利乌斯·凯撒的主要写作目的是什么？

options.en: To teach readers how to sail a boat
options.zh: 教读者如何划船

options.en: To list Roman foods
options.zh: 列出罗马的食物

options.en: To describe every single battle that was ever fought in all of Europe and Asia
options.zh: 描述在欧洲和亚洲打过的每一场战役

options.en: To show how Caesar shaped Rome's government, language, and even the calendar
options.zh: 展现凯撒如何塑造了罗马的政府、语言乃至历法

explain.en: The author wants readers to see how Caesar changed the map, government, language, and calendar of the world.
explain.zh: 作者希望读者看到凯撒如何改变了世界的版图、政府、语言与历法。

q.en: What happened to Caesar's legacy after his death?
q.zh: 凯撒死后，他的遗产如何被对待？

options.en: He was declared a god and his great-nephew Octavian became the first emperor
options.zh: 他被尊为神，其侄孙屋大维成为第一位皇帝

options.en: His name was completely forgotten by everyone except a few farmers living far out in the countryside
options.zh: 除了住在偏远乡下的几个农民外，所有人都彻底忘了他的名字

options.en: Rome returned to being ruled by kings
options.zh: 罗马又恢复了国王统治

options.en: The Senate banned all his books
options.zh: 元老院禁了他所有的书

explain.en: Caesar became a martyr and was deified; his great-nephew Octavian became Emperor Augustus, the first Roman emperor.
explain.zh: 凯撒成为殉道者并被神化；其侄孙屋大维成为第一位罗马皇帝奥古斯都。

q.en: What did crossing the Rubicon River mean for Caesar?
q.zh: 渡过卢比孔河对凯撒意味着什么？

options.en: He was going on a long vacation to rest after many years of war
options.zh: 在多年征战之后，他去度一个长长的假休息

options.en: It was an act of war against Rome
options.zh: 这是对罗马宣战的行为

options.en: He was building a bridge
options.zh: 他在建一座桥

options.en: He was visiting his farm
options.zh: 他去探望自己的农场

explain.en: Bringing his army into Italy across the Rubicon was an act of war that started the civil war.
explain.zh: 带军队越过卢比孔河进入意大利是宣战行为，由此引发内战。

prompt.en: Caesar changed Rome in many ways. Which of his actions do you think mattered most, and why?
prompt.zh: 凯撒以多种方式改变了罗马。你认为他的哪项举措最重要，为什么？

zh: 解释它如何帮助了罗马
说明它为何至今仍重要
